title: draba lactea var. glabra
intro: Draba lactea var. glabra, also known as White Whitlow Grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues, as well as possessing antioxidant properties that may help protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Draba lactea var. glabra can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often used to support overall well-being and immune function.
